title: Trajectories of Critical Unstable Qubits in and on the Bloch Sphere
abstract: We extend previous studies on a novel class of unstable two-level systems which were called Critical Unstable Qubits (CUQs). In an appropriately defined co-decaying frame, the CUQs exhibit striking phenomena of indefinite anharmonic oscillations between two states and coherence-decoherence oscillations of mixed states. These features are distinct from the usual Rabi oscillations observed in the Hermitian counterpart of two-level systems, which are harmonic and preserve the coherence of the quantum state. We employ the density matrix formalism to study these phenomena for mixed states and delve into the nature of the trajectory traversed by these states in the Bloch sphere by studying the time evolution of the Bloch vector that describes the quantum state of the unstable qubit. In particular, we provide for the first time explicit geometric constructions to obtain trajectories of both pure and mixed CUQs in and on the Bloch sphere. This enables us to identify the stationary points of CUQs, at which the states do not evolve in time in the co-decaying frame. The potential implications of our findings for particle cosmology and quantum simulations of non-Hermitian Hamiltonians are discussed.
summary: This paper analyzes the quantum dynamics of Critical Unstable Qubits (CUQs) using the density matrix formalism in a co-decaying frame. It demonstrates that CUQs exhibit unique anharmonic oscillations, distinct from standard Rabi oscillations. The authors provide novel geometric methods to map the complex trajectories of these unstable qubits onto the Bloch sphere, with implications for non-Hermitian quantum simulations.
main_problem: The paper investigates the dynamics and trajectories of a novel class of unstable two-level systems called Critical Unstable Qubits (CUQs). It aims to characterize the state evolution in the Bloch sphere for these non-Hermitian systems.
main_result: CUQ states exhibit anharmonic and coherence-decoherence oscillations in the co-decaying frame, contrasting sharply with the harmonic Rabi oscillations of stable Hermitian qubits. The authors provide explicit geometric constructions for these trajectories.
method: The analysis heavily relies on the density matrix formalism and the Bloch vector representation, solving the master equation in a specialized co-decaying frame.
model_or_system: The system is an unstable two-level qubit governed by a non-Hermitian effective Hamiltonian. The dynamics are studied using the density matrix $\rho(t)$ and its evolution in the co-decaying frame, $\hat{\rho}(t)$.
key_observables: The Bloch vector $\mathbf{b}(t)$ describes the state trajectory; key observables include the anharmonicity of oscillations and the eccentricity of the resulting elliptical paths.
important_parameters: The ratio $r = |\Gamma|/(2|E|)$ characterizes the instability, and the initial coherence $\mathbf{b}_0$ determines the specific trajectory.
main_assumptions: The analysis utilizes the Weisskopf-Wigner approximation and requires working in the co-decaying frame to maintain a physically meaningful description of coherence.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the comparison between Rabi (Hermitian) and CUQ (non-Hermitian) oscillations, showing the anharmonicity and the geometric construction of the resulting elliptical orbits on the Bloch sphere.
paper_structure: The paper progresses from defining the CUQ model and its master equation, to deriving the Bloch vector evolution equations, and finally presenting novel geometric constructions for calculating the trajectories of both pure and mixed states.
why_interesting: The study provides a detailed geometric understanding of non-Hermitian dynamics on the Bloch sphere, which is crucial for understanding open quantum systems where decay and dissipation are present, linking fundamental quantum mechanics to practical quantum simulation.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: Comparative Bloch-sphere trajectories for pure state (a) Hermitian (Rabi) and (b) non-Hermitian (CUQ) oscillations. The vectors are the evolution of the initial state b0 captured in equal time intervals. The Bloch vector rotates in the clock-wise direction. The colour grading shows the speed of the moving Bloch vector (relative to the maximum speed in the trajectory) at that time interval.

Figure 2: Comparative Bloch-sphere trajectories for mixed state Rabi and CUQ oscillations. The vectors are the evolution of the initial state b0 captured in equal time intervals. The colour grading shows the speed of the moving Bloch vector at that instant of time.
Figure 3: Bloch-sphere trajectories b(t) of the maximally-mixed CUQ for different values r.
Figure 4: Eigenvectors of the CUQ Hamiltonian on the Bloch sphere
Figure 5: Plots of b(t) with an initial mixed state b0, for different values of r.
